features.name: ADR-001 CI-Compliance Decision & product_spec §2.1 Layout Amendment
features.description: Instantiate docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md from templates/ADR_TEMPLATE.md (dogfooding the template) recording the decision to adopt CI enforcement of NDS §7 plus a repo-local compliance validator, with .github/workflows/ and tools/validate.sh as the layout addendum. Amend product_spec.md §2.1 to record both new locations, bumping its spec_version 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0 (MINOR/additive) with a matching change_log entry per NDS §4. Decision and layout of record only; no CI or validator code in this feature.
acceptance_tests: ADR file exists: test -f docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md

ADR begins with YAML frontmatter (head -1 docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md equals '---') containing all 6 required keys (id/title/description/version/status/authoritative_source each grep-present)
ADR Status line names a valid ADR status: grep -Eq '^\*\*Status:\*\* (PROPOSED|ACCEPTED|SUPERSEDED|DEPRECATED)' docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md

ADR frontmatter authoritative_source equals its own repo-relative path: grep -q 'authoritative_source: docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md' docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md
ADR body contains no unreplaced template tokens: ! grep -q '{{' docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md
product_spec.md spec_version is 1.1.0: grep -Eq 'spec_version: "1\.1\.0"' .agent/product_spec.md
product_spec.md change_log gained a 1.1.0 entry: grep -q 'version: "1.1.0"' .agent/product_spec.md
product_spec.md §2.1 references both new locations: grep -q '.github/workflows' .agent/product_spec.md && grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' .agent/product_spec.md
product_spec.md frontmatter still parses as YAML (python3 -c reads the frontmatter block between the first two '---' lines and yaml.safe_load succeeds)

features.name: tools/validate.sh Repo-Local Compliance Validator + NIZAM.json Indexing
features.description: Ship tools/validate.sh: one runtime-agnostic command, exit 0 on a clean tree and non-zero on any violation. It validates every shipped .md frontmatter against schema/frontmatter.schema.json PLUS format checks (authoritative_source equals the file's own repo-relative path or NA; status enum; semver version), runs the NDS §6.2 untagged-fence sweep, validates NIZAM.json against registry/nizam-index.schema.json and walks every indexed path, greps for branding/endpoint leakage, runs bootstrap.sh through bash -n and a timeout-guarded --help, checks module-README presence (NDS §5.3), and enforces the NDS §4 version-bump-requires-changelog rule against git history. Index tools/validate.sh in NIZAM.json (capabilities array grows; index stays schema-valid with all paths resolving). Ship negative fixtures under tools/fixtures/ (bad authoritative_source value, untagged fence, broken indexed path) that each drive a non-zero exit. Remediates G2: format is asserted, not mere key presence.
acceptance_tests: Validator exists and passes syntax: test -f tools/validate.sh && bash -n tools/validate.sh
Validator is executable: test -x tools/validate.sh
Validator exits 0 on the clean repo tree: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0
NEGATIVE bad-value fixture drives non-zero exit: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md; test $? -ne 0
NEGATIVE untagged-fence fixture drives non-zero exit: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md; test $? -ne 0
NEGATIVE broken-indexed-path fixture drives non-zero exit: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/broken_index.json; test $? -ne 0
Validator performs FORMAT (not presence) checks: grep -q 'authoritative_source' tools/validate.sh && grep -Eq 'version|semver' tools/validate.sh
tools/validate.sh is indexed in NIZAM.json: grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' NIZAM.json
NIZAM.json still validates against its schema: python3 -c 'import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open("NIZAM.json")), json.load(open("registry/nizam-index.schema.json")))'
Every path indexed in NIZAM.json resolves on disk (walker script iterates modules/capabilities/schemas/templates authoritative_source and path entries; each test -e succeeds)
Validator references its frontmatter validation target schema: grep -q 'schema/frontmatter.schema.json' tools/validate.sh

features.name: .github/workflows/compliance.yml CI Enforcement
features.description: Add .github/workflows/compliance.yml: a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tools/validate.sh on pull_request and on push to main, enforcing NDS §7 in CI. The exact command the workflow runs must also pass locally against the repo tree.
acceptance_tests: Workflow exists: test -f .github/workflows/compliance.yml
Workflow is valid YAML: python3 -c 'import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/compliance.yml"))'
Workflow triggers on pull_request and push: grep -q 'pull_request' .github/workflows/compliance.yml && grep -q 'push' .github/workflows/compliance.yml
Workflow invokes the validator: grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' .github/workflows/compliance.yml
Workflow restricts the push trigger to main: grep -Eq 'branches:\s*\[?\s*main' .github/workflows/compliance.yml || (grep -A2 'push:' .github/workflows/compliance.yml | grep -q 'main')
The exact command CI runs also passes locally: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0

features.name: Methodology & Standard Hardening (External Anchor, Evidence Capture, Plan Amendment, Release Roles)
features.description: Dogfood NDS §4 on every edit (version bump + change_log entry or root CHANGELOG line). 02_adversarial_tdd.md gains the External Anchor Rule (a consistency check MUST anchor on the source of truth, never the artifact under test — G6) and a mandate to capture one adversarial-evidence file per QA round (G3). 04_tool_driven_state.md gains the Evidence Capture Convention (exact invocation as first line, EXIT:<code> as last line, no session-absolute paths, replayable from repo root — G3) and a clock-read-timestamps rule for durable state (G4). 00_planning.md gains the Plan Amendment Rule (human-authorized post-phase amendments the orchestrator may register citing a recorded authorization event; substantive re-planning routes to the planner — G5). 05_release_train.md assigns changelog roll-up + tag mechanics explicitly to the release-manager/orchestrator role (G5).
acceptance_tests: 02_adversarial_tdd.md contains an External Anchor Rule anchoring on the source of truth: grep -Eqi 'external anchor' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md && grep -qi 'source of truth' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md

02_adversarial_tdd.md mandates a captured adversarial-evidence file per QA round: grep -qi 'adversarial' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md && grep -Eqi 'evidence file|evidence path' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md

04_tool_driven_state.md defines the Evidence Capture Convention with the invocation-first / EXIT-last shape: grep -Eqi 'evidence capture convention' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md && grep -q 'EXIT:' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md

04_tool_driven_state.md forbids session-absolute paths and mandates clock-read timestamps: grep -Eqi 'session-absolute|/tmp' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md && grep -Eqi 'clock' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md

00_planning.md defines the Plan Amendment Rule routing substantive re-planning to the planner: grep -Eqi 'plan amendment rule' methodology/00_planning.md && grep -qi 'authoriz' methodology/00_planning.md

05_release_train.md assigns changelog roll-up and tag mechanics to the release-manager/orchestrator role: grep -Eqi 'release[- ]manager|orchestrator' methodology/05_release_train.md && grep -qi 'roll-up' methodology/05_release_train.md

Each of the four edited methodology docs bumped version above 0.1.0: for f in 00_planning 02_adversarial_tdd 04_tool_driven_state 05_release_train; do grep -Eq 'version: 0\.[1-9]|version: [1-9]' methodology/$f.md || exit 1; done
Each version bump has a change record: for each edited doc a change_log entry in its own frontmatter OR a root CHANGELOG.md line naming it (validate.sh NDS §4 check passes)
All four edited docs still carry the 6 required frontmatter keys and remain schema-valid: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0

features.name: Phase Close — CHANGELOG, Green Validator, Release-Decision Human Gate
features.description: Close phase 002. Add root CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entries covering the phase-002 deliverables (CI enforcement, validator, protocol hardening). Confirm tools/validate.sh exits 0 against the final tree. Record the release decision — v0.2.0, MINOR per methodology/05_release_train.md §3.2 (new tooling + additive protocol content) — as an explicit HUMAN GATE. The pipeline does NOT cut the tag; it records that a human sign-off is required.
acceptance_tests: CHANGELOG.md has an [Unreleased] section naming the phase-002 deliverables: grep -q '## \[Unreleased\]' CHANGELOG.md && grep -Eqi 'validate\.sh|compliance|CI enforcement' CHANGELOG.md
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] references the compliance validator and CI workflow: grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' CHANGELOG.md && grep -q 'compliance.yml' CHANGELOG.md
The final tree passes the compliance validator: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0
The release decision is recorded as a human gate (v0.2.0 MINOR) and NOT auto-cut: git tag -l 'v0.2.0' | wc -l outputs 0
NIZAM.json remains schema-valid with all indexed paths resolving on the final tree (regression guard via bash tools/validate.sh exit 0)
